Prerequisite: Permission of  instructor.

title: Clinical Practices I
description: Designed to acquaint the student with the essential clinical tasks related to handling, care and treatment of small animals. Identification of dog and cat breeds. Kennel assignments and animal care outside of class are mandatory. Three hours lecture; two hours laboratory. Prerequisite: VMT 115 , VMT 216 , Corequisite: MAT 103 . (4 sem. hrs.)

title: Veterinary Anatomy/Physiology
description: Study of the normal structures and functions of  the animal body. Species studied will include the  dog, cat, horse, cow, sheep,goat and pig. Four  hours lecture; two hours laboratory.  Prerequisites: BIO 101 or 110 and Corequisite: CHM 101 or 103 and MAT 103 . (5 sem. hrs.)

title: Clinical Practices I Lab
description: Laboratory component of VMT213: Clinical Practices I.

title: Non-Infectious Diseases and Nutrition
description: Study of animal non-infectious diseases with emphasis on disease control, zoonosis, interpersonal and client education, and nutritional support of diseased animals. Three hours lecture. Prerequisite: VMT 115 and VMT 216 . (3 sem. hrs)

title: Cervantes' Don Quijote de la Mancha
description: Close reading and discussion of Don Quijote. Background lectures will depict the contexts (historical, social, cultural) in which Cervantes wrote his masterpiece. Additional readings will acquaint students with some of the most important criticism relating to Don Quijote, particularly its role as perhaps the first metafictional modern novel. Prerequisite: Permission of instructor.

description: Laboratory component of VMT220: Clinical Practices  II.

title: Clinical Practices II
description: The essential clinical tasks related to handling,  care and treatment of large animals and  radiographic examination of both large and small  animals, with emphasis on radiation safety and  methods of obtaining high-quality diagnostic  radiographs. Barn assignments and animal care  outside of class is mandatory. Three hours  lecture; three hours laboratory. Prerequisites:  213 and 216. (4 sem. hrs.)

title: Clinical Practices II
description: The essential clinical tasks related to handling, care and treatment of large animal. Identification of various breeds of horses and livestock. Barn assignments and animal care outside of class is mandatory. Three hours lecture; three hours laboratory. Prerequisites: VMT 213 , VMT 216 , Proof of pre-exposure Rabies vaccine. (4 sem. hrs.)

title: Clinical Practices II Lab
description: Lab component of VMT-221 :  Clinical Practices II

title: Veterinary Radiology
description: The essential clinical tasks related to the radiographic examination of both large and small animals, with emphasis on radiation safety and methods of obtaining high-quality diagnostic radiographs. 7 week course. Three hours lecture; three hours laboratory. Prerequisites: VMT 213 and VMT 216 . (2 sem. hrs.)

title: Vet Anatomy/Physiology Lab
description: Laboratory component of VMT-216 : Veterinary Anatomy & Physiology.

title: Laboratory Techniques Lab
description: Laboratory component of VMT312: Laboratory Techniques.

title: Animal Diseases & Nutrition
description: Study of animal diseases with emphasis on disease control, zoonoses, client education and  nutritional support of diseased animals.  Prerequisite: VMT 216 .

title: Laboratory Techniques
description: Study of principles and practices of clinical pathology as they relate to responsibilities of veterinary medical technicians. Includes hematological techniques, fecal and urine examination, blood chemistries, serologic testing, vaginal smears and semen evaluation. Three hours lecture; three hours laboratory. Prerequisite: VMT 213 . (4 sem. hrs.)

title: Veterinary Clinical Pathology
description: Study of principles and practices of clinical pathology as they relate to responsibilities of veterinary nurses. Includes hematological techniques, urine examination, blood chemistries, serologic testing, veterinary microbiology, immunology, and cytology. Three hours lecture; three hours laboratory. Prerequisite: VMT 216 and CHM 101 or CHM 103 .  Pre/Corequisite: VMT 213 (4 sem. hrs.)

title: Veterinary Clinical Pathology Lab
description: Lab component of Veterinary Clinical Pathology

title: Pharmacology Lab
description: Laboratory component of VMT318: Pharmacology.

title: Laboratory Animal Science Lab
description: Laboratory Component of VMT320: Laboratory Animal Science.

title: Veterinary Radiology Lab
description: Lab component of Veterinary Radiology

title: Pharmacology I Lab
description: Lab Component of VMT-321 :  Pharmacology I

title: Pharmacology I
description: A study of the basic principles of pharmacology including pharmacodyamics and pharmacokinetics, dosage calculations, drugs affecting the nervous and cardiovascular systems, anesthesiology, and drugs used in pain management. Anesthetic drugs and anesthesia procedures will be emphasized in the laboratory. Animal care outside of class is mandatory. Three hours lecture for 15 weeks; three hours laboratory for 7 weeks. Prerequisites: BIO 101 or 110; MAT 103 ; CHM 101 or 103; VMT 213 ; 216; VMT 313 --must be taken either prior to or at the same time as this course. (4 sem. hrs.)

title: Topics in VMT
description: Upper level courses offered on an occasional basis in areas of veterinary science.

title: Pharmacology II
description: A continuation of the study of drugs commonly used in veterinary clinical practice including those used to treat inflammatory, respiratory, infectious, gastro-intestinal, dermatologic, ophthalmic and endocrine diseases.  Two hours lecture. Prerequisites: VMT 213 ; VMT216; VMT 321 ; BIO 101 or 110; MAT 103 ; CHM 101 or 103. Corequisite: VMT 217 . (2 sem. hrs.)

title: Advanced Equine Techniques
description: This course will involve equine radiology, ultrasonography, advanced techniques in sample collection and processing, management of disease conditions, treatment of injuries and nutrient management during recovery from such conditions. Three hours lecture; three hours laboratory. (4 sem. hrs.) Course prerequisite: EFT 213 , EQS 225 , VMT 221 ; Prerequisite or Co-requisite VMT 222

title: Veterinary Business Management
description: This course will focus on the business management concepts in relation to veterinary practices. Students will be challenged to take on the role of a practice manager through course material and practical application. Topics will include inventory management, personnel roles and scheduling, conflict resolution, veterinary law, and ethics. Prerequisite: BUS 124 , VMT 115 , VMT 213 ,and VMT 216 .  (3 sem. hrs)

title: Pharmacology
description: Study of drugs and their effects on animals, including principles of drug action, drug forms, usage, dosage and solution problems, toxicity, dispensing procedures and legal considerations. Anesthetic drugs and principles of anesthesiology will be emphasized in the laboratory. Animal care outside of class is mandatory. Three lecture; three hours laboratory. Prerequisites: VMT 213 , 216, and concurrent enrollment in VMT 312 ; BIO 101 or 110; MAT 103 ; CHM 101 or 103. (4 sem. hrs.)

title: Veterinary Internship
description: Practical full-time work experience in a veterinary practice or facility approved by the program director. 240 hours required. Prerequisites: VMT 115 , VMT 209 , VMT 216 , VMT213, VMT 221 , VMT 222 , VMT 313 , Proof of pre-exposure Rabies vaccine.

title: Laboratory Animal Science
description: Introduction to laboratory animals most commonly used in research labs and drug companies, including laboratory animal care, identification procedures, housing, sanitation, diseases and parasites of laboratory animals. Laboratory animal care outside of class is mandatory. Three hours lecture; two hours laboratory.  Prerequisites: VMT 216 , VMT 213 , VMT 312 or VMT 313 . (4 sem. hrs.)

title: Feminist Theories & Perspectives
description: Examination of recent studies of women's  experiences that have questioned traditional understanding of human nature, sexuality, social change, psychological development, political behavior, the family and creativity. Explores of topics in various disciplines from a women centered perspective, emphasizing recent feminist debates in anthropology, psychology, law, history, literature, medicine and health, and politics. WS, CD

title: Equine Clinical & Surgical Practices
description: This course involves the principles of general anesthesia and surgery in equine practice. Surgeries commonly performed in equine practice, the role of the veterinary technologist in the OR, pre- and post-surgical care for the horse, and the importance of proper home care after hospitalization will be stressed. Prerequisite: VMT 330 . (3 sem. hrs.)

title: Veterinary Surgical Nursing
description: Planned clinical experience to enhance technical competence. Emphasis placed on surgical nursing and anesthesia skills. Surgical assignments and nursing care outside of class are mandatory. One five-hour session per week. Prerequisites: VMT 115 , VMT 209 , VMT 216 , VMT 213 , VMT 221 , VMT 222 , VMT 313 , VMT 321 , Proof of pre-exposure Rabies vaccine (4 sem. hrs.)

title: Women in Science
description: The role of women in scientific research and their contributions to scientific innovations; the struggle and lack of recognition of women; the movement toward acceptance in the scientific world; and the careers of women scientists. Students are required to research a woman scientist and present their findings in written and oral presentations. WS, WI

title: Topics in Women Studies
description: Courses emphasizing feminist scholarship offered on an irregular basis to meet student interests and needs.

title: Advanced Equine Techniques Lab
description: Laboratory component of VMT-330 : Advanced Equine Techniques.

title: Topics in VMT
description: Upper level courses offered on an occasional basis in areas of veterinary science, such as VMT 370 : Clinical Animal Behavior.

title: Topics in Women Studies
description: A seminar course offered as needed to cover topics of interest to students and faculty which are not treated in the regular curriculum. Prerequisite: permission of instructor.

title: Feminist Theory: Visual Culture
description: A study of feminist theory that explores the mutually constructive relationship between gender identities and visual cultures; analyzes the way in which feminist insights drive many modes of understanding visual culture; and recognizes that visual experience is one of the key modes by which gender is culturally inscribed. Prerequisites: Permission of Master of Humanities director.

title: Feminist Theory: Literary Analysis
description: A study of feminist theory that explores how women have been able to actively author texts despite being considered passive objects by patriarchal authority, thus analyzing how creative literary expression has enabled these women to literally write their subjectivity into existence. Prerequisites: English Foundations course and one 200-level course in any discipline. WS, LIT, WI
